markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Ensure indexing, tests, visual capture, and backend nav do not steal focus or pop foreground windows. This MT belongs to ParallelSwarmStateRecovery. Make WP-009 safe for local/cloud swarm work, backend navigation, and repeated session recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Local and cloud swarm lanes must be observable, attributable, bounded, recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Every MT must leave enough receipts, state markers, and next-step context for session-limit recovery by a no-context model.
scope.expected_behavior: QuietModeBackgroundWork leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: QuietModeBackgroundWork is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

commits.slice: MT-219 quiet indexing, test, visual capture, and backend navigation work

evidence.summary: MT-219 adds PostgreSQL/EventLedger quiet-background-work receipts, a RunQuietBackgroundWork capability, constrained quiet policies for indexing/backend-navigation/visual-capture/test-run work, quiet indexing lease acquisition that avoids queued orphan leases under contention, quiet code-index/check/screenshot wrappers that preflight denial paths before side effects, backend navigation route receipt emission, and DB constraints enforcing quiet-policy booleans and work-kind alignment.

commands.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests quiet -- --nocapture
commands.result: PASS; 4/4 quiet-background-work focused PostgreSQL/EventLedger tests passed
PASS; 24/24 parallel swarm state recovery tests passed
PASS; 2/2 backend navigation API tests passed with persisted quiet receipt assertions

evidence.summary: Reviewer confirmed the earlier ordering and orphan gaps are closed: quiet indexing uses try_acquire_indexing_lease before receipt/KIR write, visual capture prevalidates request/focus/PNG/redaction before receipt, quiet checks preflight missing capability and unsupported kind before receipt while using the planned evidence artifact id, and backend nav routes persist and return quiet receipt IDs. Residual uncertainty is limited to infrastructure-level cross-transaction failure, not an in-scope MT-219 implementation gap.

Reviewer found no concrete in-scope MT-219 gap under the narrowed quiet swarm/background contract. They verified quiet wrappers call real KIR/artifact/check code, successful backend nav routes persist receipts, denial paths occur before KIR/artifact/check side effects, and normal foreground operator/CLI paths were not treated as blockers because no in-scope quiet swarm/background bypass was found.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-219 requires quiet/non-intrusive background work (indexing, test runs, visual capture, backend navigation) for parallel swarm lanes with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The commit 1e55f9d6 delivers a real implementation: a new PG table with CHECK constraints and an EventLedger FK, a RunQuietBackgroundWork capability, a quiet policy enforced in DB and code, and quiet wrappers that preflight denial paths before side effects. Proof runs against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL, not mocks.
findings: Migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0313_parallel_swarm_quiet_background_work.sql: real PostgreSQL table knowledge_agent_quiet_background_work with work_kind CHECK (indexing/backend_navigation/visual_capture/test_run), a quiet-policy CHECK enforcing all five quiet booleans, receipt_id LIKE 'PSR-QUIET-%' constraint, FK event_ledger_event_id REFERENCES kernel_event_ledger(event_id), plus a quiet_policy_jsonb column + CHECK added to knowledge_parallel_indexing_lease_queue, and a knowledge_schema_registry insert. Native PG authority, not SQLite/sidecar.

state_recovery.rs: adds RunQuietBackgroundWork capability granted to non-Validator lanes (Validator lanes excluded), QuietBackgroundPolicy with no_foreground_window/no_focus_steal/no_os_shell_window/bounded/observable, record_quiet_background_work() appending a KnowledgeQuietBackgroundWorkRecorded EventLedger event in-tx then inserting the receipt row, validate_quiet_background_policy() rejecting any non-quiet flag, and try_acquire_indexing_lease() returning None on contention/unique-violation instead of queueing an orphan lease. require_capability(RunQuietBackgroundWork) gates writes.

engine.rs start_quiet_run(), governance_check_runner.rs run_quiet_check(), product_screenshot_capture.rs record_native_product_screenshot_quiet(), and api/knowledge_code_nav.rs record_quiet_nav_receipt() all preflight capability/lease/kind/focus/PNG/redaction validation BEFORE recording the quiet receipt or performing product side effects. product_screenshot_capture.rs carries the HBR-QUIET console-window suppression tie-in. This closes the prior adversarial ordering/orphan concerns recorded in the MT evidence block.

Proof realism: tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:1-24 documents and enforces real-PostgreSQL-only via ManagedPostgres::ensure_running (no Docker, no external daemon, explicit 'There is NO SQLite, in-memory, or mock fallback'); absent binaries -> loud SKIP. parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests.rs exercises real record_quiet_background_work/start_quiet_run/run_quiet_check/resolve_backend_navigation_quiet, asserts EventLedger event_type KNOWLEDGE_QUIET_BACKGROUND_WORK_RECORDED, persisted-row counts, loud-policy rejection persists 0 rows, validator-write rejection, and quiet_entrypoint_denials_happen_before_product_side_effects proves denied index leaves 0 knowledge_index_runs rows and contention leaves no orphan queued lease.

knowledge_code_nav_api_tests.rs exercises the real backend-navigation HTTP routes end-to-end and asserts each response returns a quiet_background_work_receipt_id whose knowledge_agent_quiet_background_work row exists in PG (assert_backend_nav_quiet_receipt).

Spec alignment: current Master Spec v02.194 module 05 (lines 1765-1870) confirms the HBR-QUIET and HBR-SWARM families (sandbox quietness, no-foreground/no-focus-steal, bounded/observable parallel work) backed by HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json; the quiet policy fields map directly onto these gates. Aside (non-blocking, expected): packet cites v02.192 anchors while current is v02.194.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
